title: eriogonum strictum var. anserinum
intro: Eriogonum strictum var. anserinum, commonly known as desert buckwheat, is a perennial wildflower native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of desert buckwheat include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and support the immune system. Desert buckwheat can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
